examples: The landscape, brown and sere beneath the sun.
The trees begin to proclaim the change. Soon the leaves will be discarded, the grass will sere.
These effects are clear in Morgan's studies of cognitive performance during SERE training.
"If we're tasked to execute this type of mission, doesn't matter what service, male, female, they're our brothers and sisters in arms, " SERE specialist "Jesse" said.
The sere, crumbly atmosphere of a dig, as I envisioned it, seemed a welcome relief from the maelstrom I now perceived to be teeming within the human body.
